description: Column-level schema changes to the DataDawn OpenRegs database, newest first. If a previously-working query now returns HTTP 400 ("Cannot sort table by X" or "no such column"), a column it references may have been dropped or renamed below. Invalid-sort requests return 400 (client error), not 500.

changes.title: FEC contributions vs independent-expenditure fix

added_columns.note: FEC itpas2 transaction type, restored after an earlier build dropped it. 24K/24Z = contributions received by the candidate; 24A = independent expenditure AGAINST; 24E = IE FOR; 24C/24F/24N = coordinated/communication. RULE: never SUM(transaction_amt) over fec_contributions without filtering transaction_tp.
Independent expenditures FOR the member (transaction_tp 24E). Outside spending, NOT money received.
Independent expenditures AGAINST the member (transaction_tp 24A). Opposition outside spending, NOT money received.

changed_semantics.change: Recomputed to sum ONLY transaction_tp 24K+24Z (PAC/party contributions actually received). Previously summed ALL transaction types, ~79% of which are independent expenditures (51% spent AGAINST the member) — e.g. Mark Kelly showed $117.3M vs $2.5M actually received. Values are now much lower for battleground members and correct.

changes.aggregation_guidance: Money a candidate RECEIVED from committees: SUM(fec_contributions.transaction_amt) WHERE transaction_tp IN ('24K','24Z'). Independent expenditures FOR use 24E, AGAINST use 24A. Never sum across all transaction types under a 'received' label.

changes.title: Lobbying schema fix

dropped_columns.reason: Replicated filing-level amount across every activity row; SUM over-counted by activity-count (2-3x for many clients).
Was a COALESCE of two distinct concepts (firm income vs in-house expense).

changes.aggregation_guidance: To aggregate lobbying spend: JOIN lobbying_activities to lobbying_filings ON filing_uuid, SUM the filing-level columns, and filter to LD-2 quarterly activity reports with WHERE filing_type GLOB '[1234Q]*' (LD-203 contribution reports carry a semantically different income field and would double-count).
